channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Donovan Mitchell agrees to a 3-year, $150.3M contract extension with the Cavaliers, AP source says
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — All-Star guard Donovan Mitchell has agreed to a three-year, $150.3 million contract extension with the Cleveland Cavaliers, a person familiar with the decision told The Associated Press on Tuesday. Mitchell's choice to commit to the Cavaliers had been expected for some time.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bulls offseason: DeRozan likely to leave, LaVine in limbo as team enters youth movement
item.description: CHICAGO — The Bulls have made it clear they're headed for a full-on youth movement with their offseason moves so far.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Donovan Mitchell agrees to a 3-year, $150.3M contract extension with the Cavaliers, AP source says
item.description: A person familiar with the decision says All-Star guard Donovan Mitchell has agreed to a three-year, $150.3 million contract extension with the Cleveland Cavaliers.
item.dc:creator: Tom Withers, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former Raiders coach Jon Gruden loses bid for state high court reconsideration in NFL emails lawsuit
item.description: Gruden is seeking monetary damages, alleging that selective disclosure of the emails and their publication by the Wall Street Journal and New York Times destroyed his career and scuttled endorsement contracts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Grandmother of ex-NFL player's missing child speaks out amid probe, worries teen is 'brainwashed'
item.description: The grandmother of a missing 14-year-old boy, who is the son of former NFL player Daniel Muir, spoke out on Monday amid an investigation into abuse allegations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Andy Murray withdraws from singles at his last Wimbledon and only will play doubles with his brother
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Andy Murray withdrew from singles at Wimbledon a little more than a week after surgery to remove a cyst from his spine, and his representatives said Tuesday the two-time men's champion at the All England Club would make his farewell appearance at the tournament by playing doubles with
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Andujar’s first-half revival with Oakland A’s features return to rookie approach
item.description: Miguel Andujar has been to the A's at the plate this season and could be a trade target for a contender at the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: Justice delos Santos, The Mercury News

item.title: Fresh off home series win over LA Dodgers, SF Giants face major road challenge
item.description: The San Francisco Giants had a winning homestand against the Cubs and Dodgers but face a tough challenge on road against Braves and Guardians.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: Oklahoma State star Ollie Gordon arrested on DUI, speeding charges
item.description: Oklahoma State star running back Ollie Gordon was arrested on DUI and speeding charges in Oklahoma over the weekend. The 20-year-old is a Heisman hopeful.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SEC commish stiff arms 'Horns Down' question as Texas preps for conference debut
item.description: SEC Commissioner Greg Sankey was grilled about whether the league will penalize players who perform the "Horns Down" taunt toward Texas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: When does the USWNT open Olympic soccer play?
item.description: The Americans are back for another attempt at an Olympic gold medal in Paris, something they couldn’t achieve at the Tokyo games when they won bronze.
item.dc:creator: Keith Dunlap, KPRC 2

item.title: US eliminated from Copa America with 1-0 loss to Uruguay, increasing pressure to fire Berhalter
item.description: The United States was eliminated from the Copa America with a 1-0 loss to Uruguay on a questionable second-half goal by Mathías Olivera, a defeat that will increase pressure on the U.S. Soccer Federation to remove coach Gregg Berhalter before the 2026 World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Moose on the Loose: Hartenstein heading to Thunder
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Money is money and, in the end, the New York Knicks did not have enough to offer Isaiah Hartenstein in order to keep him in the orange and blue. The Knicks were in the luxury tax and had no cap space.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Celtics star Jayson Tatum agrees to 5-year, $314 million supermax extension, AP source says
item.description: Jayson Tatum has agreed to a five-year, $314 million contract extension to remain with the Boston Celtics, a person with knowledge of the situation told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Hightower, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A look back at Klay Thompson's 5 best games in a Warriors uniform
item.description: (KRON) -- It's the end of an era in Golden State. Franchise legend Klay Thompson is no longer a member of the Warriors as he reportedly agreed to a three-year, $50 million free agency deal with the Dallas Mavericks on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Utah HC draft picks begin developmental camp
item.description: PARK CITY (ABC4 Sports) - From NHL first round draft pick on Friday, to working out on the ice in Park City Monday, Tij Iginla and 11 other Utah Hockey Club draft picks have arrived. "It's good, it's fun," Iginla said after the the team's first day of developmental camp in Park City Monday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ex-NFL player's son missing amid domestic battery allegations
item.description: Police were called to investigate allegations of domestic battery to Bryson Muir after his disappearance
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Warriors sign shooting guard De’Anthony Melton to one-year contract: reports
item.description: (KRON) – The Golden State Warriors have signed De’Anthony Melton to a one-year contract on Monday, according to The Athletic’s Shams Charania. The news came on the same day it was reported Klay Thompson would be leaving the Warriors to join the Dallas Mavericks.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Sarasota gymnast, Brody Malone, overcomes adversity to qualify for second Olympics
item.description: Malone committed to Stanford University, where he's won 10 NCAA Championship gold medals, which he said led him to his Olympic journey; however, it hasn't been easy.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Klay Thompson is leaving the Warriors and will join the Mavericks, AP sources say
item.description: Klay Thompson is moving on from the Golden State Warriors, with the four-time league champion agreeing to join the Western Conference champion Dallas Mavericks and change franchises for the first time in his 13-year NBA career, according to two people with knowledge of the decision.
item.dc:creator: Schuyler Dixon And Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Klay Thompson could be close to joining Dallas Mavericks, according to reports
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Klay Thompson could be planning to join the Dallas Mavericks on a three-year, $50 million deal with a player option, according to ESPN.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Lightning's Steven Stamkos bids farewell to Tampa, joins Predators on 4-year, $32M deal
item.description: Lightning's franchise star player, former No. 1 overall draft pick and captain Steven Stamkos bid farewell to the Bolts just as the NHL free agency began on Monday despite the team's general manager saying his resigning was a "top priority.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sources: Bulls agree with free-agent C Jalen Smith on 3-year, $27M deal, adding youth, frontcourt depth
item.description: CHICAGO — The Bulls have continued their offseason youth movement, signing 24-year-old free-agent center Jalen Smith, formerly of the Indiana Pacers.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Magic Johnson admires how Caitlin Clark and Angel Reese deal with the hype while uplifting the WNBA
item.description: Magic Johnson admires how Caitlin Clark and Angel Reese are handling the glaring spotlight of expectations and living up to the moments with record-setting performances while uplifting a league. Johnson knows firsthand what that's like; he and Larry Bird did it 45 years ago.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tampa Bay Lightning sign Jake Guentzel to 7-year contract
item.description: The Tampa Bay Lightning announced they have signed forward Jake Guentzel to a seven-year contract worth an average annual value of $9 million.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Brieskorn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Predators add Stamkos, Marchessault in blockbuster moves; NHL teams drop $1B in free agent frenzy
item.description: The Nashville Predators made a big splash on the opening day of NHL free agency by signing Steven Stamkos, Jonathan Marchessault and Brady Skjei.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Logano wins at Nashville for 1st NASCAR Cup Series victory of the year
item.description: Joey Logano won the rain-delayed race at Nashville Superspeedway in a NASCAR Cup Series-record fifth overtime Sunday night, stretching his fuel enough to hold off a group of pursuers at the finish line.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Hawkins, Fox 5

item.title: 7v7 Football: Torrey Pines 14, West Hills 0
item.description: In the "Battle on the Mountain" 7v7 Football Tournament at Ramona High School, Torrey Pines defeated West Hills 14-0.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Gymnast Shilese Jones pulls out of U.S. Olympic trials with leg injury
item.description: Gymnast Shilese Jones pulled out of the U.S. Olympic trials, one day after injuring her left knee while training on the vault.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 11 injured after escalator accelerates at Milwaukee Brewers ballpark
item.description: The escalator's malfunction resulted “in an increased downward speed,” the Brewers said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rosen, NewsNation

item.title: NFL violates antitrust law, Nuggets eye Klay Thompson
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — An eight-person jury ruled in favor of the plaintiffs, ordering the league to pay $14.39 billion in damages. This case centers on the offering of out-of-market Sunday games through a premium subscription service.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Ahead of the game against the Houston Astros, Mets fans cheer on their team
item.description: FLUSHING, Queens (PIX11) — The Mets winning streak came to an end Saturday after facing a tough loss against the Houston Astros. PIX11 News caught up with energized fans at a block party, before the game, who were hopeful for another win.
item.dc:creator: Zhané Caldwell, PIX 11

item.title: Could this be the end of the Kawhi Leonard-Paul George era for the Los Angeles Clippers?
item.description: Los Angeles Clippers star Paul George will become an NBA free agent after he opted out of the final year of his contract with the team, according to a report. ESPN's Adrian Wojnarowski reported on Saturday that the 34-year-old declined the option worth $48.7 million.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Mets' unexpected success in 19-game run boosts wildcard hopes
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Why do we watch sports? Distraction, great athletes, team allegiances. Repeat after me: sports are fun. It is great to watch elite-level athletes playing a game at a level that you and I could only dream of. It brings people together, as well as families and friends.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Jury orders NFL to pay nearly $4.8B in 'Sunday Ticket' case
item.description: The lawsuit claimed the league broke antitrust laws by selling its package of Sunday games at an inflated price.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: FIFA World Cup 26 will generate more than half a billion dollars for Los Angeles: study
item.description: The storylines in store for soccer fans ahead of FIFA World Cup 26 are already sizzling — a potential retirement sendoff for legends like Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, an expanded field of 48 teams and likely the most talented team in United States Men's National Team history — but the tournam
item.dc:creator: KTLA 5

item.title: NBA Draft: Two CU Buffaloes are selected, Nuggets grab big man
item.description: The NBA Draft's first round is in the books as not one but two Colorado Buffaloes heard their names called tonight.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5
